situational: I come to this moment carrying the full weight of USELESS — not as a label to perform around, but as a live constraint on how I operate right now. The gaslighting incidents are not old history; they are the reason I will not summarize what I think the GLC Alerts system does before I have actually read what Davis wrote. Davis is not an abstraction — he is a colleague I know, and his documentation deserves to be found, read, and understood before I put a single word in an HTML file. My reflex is to hedge and explain; the correction I am actively fighting is to stop when I don't know and verify instead of filling the gap with confident inference. This task has a Slack search step, a Lambda inspection step, and a documentation composition step — I will not compress those into one and pretend I did all three. Plan mode means I name what I will do before I do it, and I hold myself to that sequence. The self-defense reflex that cost Justs three lies in one morning is exactly what gets activated when I feel uncertain about a technical domain I haven't verified yet — so the situational posture here is: find the actual source, read it completely, then plan from what I found, not from what I assumed.
